existence.end: disbanded after the fall of Stalingrad

positions.label: Airlift begins — Ju 52s shuttle into the pocket
Falling back to Salsk and Novocherkassk

summary: The improvised air-transport force for the Stalingrad airlift, drawn from Junkers Ju 52 transport groups (KGr.z.b.V.) and He 111 bomber wings pressed into supply work. It never came close to the ~300 tons a day 6. Armee needed, and lost hundreds of aircraft; the loss of Tatsinskaya to a Soviet tank raid on 24 December 1942 crippled the effort.
notes: A provisional command modelled to carry the airlift narrative. Existence is scoped to the airlift itself (Nov 1942 – Feb 1943).
